name: Cajun Catfish with Spicy Strawberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Use strawberry-flavored vinegar instead of red wine vinegar if desired.
2. Place fillets in a large shallow dish. Season the fish with salt, black pepper, and hot pepper sauce; cover and refrigerate for 1 hour.
3. In a small saucepan, combine preserves, vinegar, soy sauce, cocktail sauce, minced garlic, and horseradish. Simmer the sauce over low heat, stirring occasionally while preparing the catfish.
4. In a shallow bowl, blend cornmeal and flour.
5. Drain the catfish and dredge it in the cornmeal mixture, coating all sides.
6. Heat oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. When hot, add the catfish and sauté until browned on both sides. Drain well on paper towels; keep warm.
7. Spoon 1/4 cup sauce onto each plate. Top with catfish fillets. Garnish with sliced strawberries and parsley if desired.
